chunk_summary: This section focuses on severe anemia triage and transfusion referral decisions under the Anemia Mukt Bharat program, emphasizing clinical guidelines, referral protocols, and management strategies for severe anemia cases in India.
learning_objectives: Understand the triage and referral protocols for severe anemia under Anemia Mukt Bharat.
Learn the clinical thresholds for blood transfusion in severe anemia cases.
Identify key guidelines for managing severe anemia in children and pregnant women.
Explore the role of national programs in addressing severe anemia through fortified nutrition and clinical interventions.
key_facts: Severe anemia is defined as hemoglobin levels <7 g/dL in pregnant women and <6 g/dL in children (WHO guidelines).
Immediate referral for blood transfusion is recommended for hemoglobin <4 g/dL or symptomatic anemia.
The T3 guideline under Anemia Mukt Bharat emphasizes triage, treatment, and tracking of anemia cases.
Iron supplementation and fortified foods are critical components of anemia prevention strategies.
The Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) recommends early diagnosis and treatment to prevent complications in children.
WHO guidelines stress the importance of hospital-based care for severe anemia in children, including transfusion protocols.
reference_quote: "Immediate blood transfusion is recommended for hemoglobin levels <4 g/dL or in cases of symptomatic severe anemia." (Source: WHO Pocket Book of Hospital Care for Children)
document_key_topics: Severe anemia triage and referral protocols
Iron deficiency management in children and pregnant women
